sourceName: University of Hong Kong Career Guidance
resumeLength: Prefer one page for students and early-career roles; allow two pages only when the user's experience is substantial and highly relevant.

formattingRules.fontRecommendation: Readable professional font, 10-11pt body, clear section headings
formattingRules.margin: Use balanced margins and enough white space for dense but readable Hong Kong applications
formattingRules.bulletStyle: Concise bullets with consistent indentation and strong first verbs

writingRules.tone: Professional, concise, evidence-based, suitable for Hong Kong internships, graduate schemes, finance, consulting, business and local employer screening
writingRules.bulletFormula: Action verb + business/academic context + method/tool + result or learning outcome
quantificationRules: Use numbers for transaction size, users, respondents, reports, frequency, accuracy, time saved, revenue, cost or ranking only when supported by user facts.
For Hong Kong finance, consulting and business roles, prioritize analytical scope, stakeholder context, client/business impact and bilingual or cross-border relevance when true.
Do not invent grades, awards, company names, deal values or employment outcomes.

exampleBullets.weak: Helped with market research for a finance project.
exampleBullets.strong: Analyzed 30+ comparable companies and summarized valuation drivers in Excel, supporting a market-entry recommendation for the student finance project.
exampleBullets.reason: Makes scope, method and business use clear without inventing unsupported impact.

exampleBullets.strong: Coordinated a 6-person team to plan a campus career event, managing speaker outreach, registration tracking and on-site operations.
exampleBullets.reason: Shows leadership, coordination and operational detail relevant to Hong Kong graduate applications.

scoringCriteria.description: Aligns resume structure and language with Hong Kong internships, graduate schemes and local employer screening.
Uses concrete scope, tools, stakeholders and supported outcomes.
Prioritizes finance, consulting, business, technology or education evidence according to the selected role.
Keeps the resume readable, ATS-friendly and suitable for PDF submission.
Avoids inflated language and unsupported metrics.

promptSnippets.title: Hong Kong resume guidance
promptSnippets.content: For Hong Kong applications, combine international resume clarity with Hong Kong employer expectations: compact one-page structure, strong education and internship evidence, role-specific keywords, supported metrics, and business/finance/consulting context when relevant.
